user_message: Can you work with me on executive brief for Consolidated Messenger? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-01-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-O trap (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-O failure mode (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-O -- Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
